content.text:
```json
[
  {
    "idea_id": "value-quality-rotation",
    "title": "Value-Quality Factor Rotation",
    "thesis": "Rotate between value and quality equity factors based on relative valuation regimes and earnings yield spreads. When value is cheap relative to quality (high P/E spread), overweight value; when quality is expensive, rotate to quality. This exploits mean-reversion in factor premiums and avoids drawdown concentration in either factor.",
    "data_sources": [
      "yfinance (daily OHLCV, quarterly financials via info endpoint)",
      "FRED (10-year Treasury yield for risk-free rate)"
    ],
    "signal_logic": "Calculate trailing 12-month P/E and earnings yield for value universe (QQQ proxy) and quality universe (VTV proxy). Compute the P/E spread: PE_quality - PE_value. If spread > historical 75th percentile, value is cheap; if spread < 25th percentile, quality is cheap. Confirm with earnings yield differential: if earnings_yield_value - earnings_yield_quality > 0.5%, increase conviction.",
    "entry_exit": "Buy signal: spread exceeds 75th percentile and earnings yield spread > 0.5% for 2 consecutive weeks. Sell signal: spread reverts to median or drops below 50th percentile, OR position held for 12 weeks without confirmation. Rebalance monthly.",
    "position_sizing": "Allocate 60% to favored factor, 40% to alternative. Use inverse Kelly-style adjustment: position size = min(0.05 * spread_zscore, 0.30) of portfolio per factor.",
    "universe": [
      "VTV",
      "QQQ",
      "VUG"
    ],
    "frequency": "weekly",
    "risks": "Factor regimes can persist for years (e.g., 2010-2020 growth dominance), making mean-reversion trades costly. Spread calculation requires reliable earnings data; yfinance financials may lag or be incomplete. High turnover and slippage during rotations. Does not hedge tail risk; add protective put or reduce leverage in high-volatility regimes.",
    "implementation_notes": "1. Fetch historical price and earnings data for VTV/QQQ/VUG via yfinance. 2. Calculate rolling 12-month P/E using trailing earnings. 3. Compute z-score of P/E spread vs. 252-day rolling window. 4. Generate weekly signals when z-score crosses ±1.5 threshold. 5. Track position entry date and holding duration. 6. Implement position sizing via DataFrame logic (zscore * kelly_fraction). 7. Backtest with 5% slippage per trade and monthly rebalance friction.",
    "required_keys": []
  }
]
```
